name: sql-to-osc
description:
SQL to OSC (Online Schema Change) conversion expert for Flyway migration scripts.
Use when: (1) Converting SQL migration files to OSC format, (2) User mentions "OSC", "轉換 OSC", "osc.txt", or "Online Schema Change", (3) Working with Flyway ALTER TABLE/CREATE INDEX statements that need OSC conversion.
capabilities: Parse Flyway SQL migration files from standard location
Extract database name, table name, and operations from SQL statements
Convert ALTER TABLE statements to OSC format
Transform CREATE INDEX to ADD INDEX syntax
Format output as TAB-separated OSC lines
Generate conversion summary with operation statistics
author: DennisLiuCk
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based documentation skill with no executable code. The static analyzer produced false positives by misidentifying metadata and documentation elements as security threats. The 39 flagged 'issues' are benign: SHA-256 hashes identified as 'weak crypto', git refs as 'C2 keywords', and markdown code examples as 'external commands'. This skill only transforms text following documented patterns - it makes no network calls, executes no scripts, and accesses no filesystem beyond what the AI assistant controls.
valueStatement: Database migrations using Flyway require conversion for online schema changes. This skill transforms standard SQL migration scripts into OSC format for zero-downtime deployments.
userTitle: Convert SQL migrations to OSC format
limitations: Does not validate SQL syntax or schema correctness
Only processes standard Flyway migration file patterns
Does not execute migrations or connect to databases
Requires manual review before production deployment

useCases.title: Prepare zero-downtime migrations
useCases.description: Convert Flyway SQL scripts to OSC format for online schema changes without downtime

useCases.title: Automate migration conversion
useCases.description: Transform database migration scripts automatically as part of deployment pipelines

useCases.title: Standardize OSC output
useCases.description: Ensure consistent OSC format across database migration files and teams

promptTemplates.title: Basic conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this SQL migration to OSC format. Read the file and output to the standard osc directory.

promptTemplates.title: With summary
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this SQL migration file to OSC and provide a summary of affected tables and operations performed.

promptTemplates.title: Batch conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert all SQL migration files in the migration directory to OSC format for today.

promptTemplates.title: Format verification
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this SQL to OSC format and verify the output meets UTF-8 encoding, TAB separators, and proper line endings.

bestPractices: Always review OSC output before deployment to catch any missed conversions
Use today's date in osc filename to maintain proper version ordering
Keep original SQL migration files as audit trail alongside OSC output
antiPatterns: Skipping manual review of OSC output before production deployment
Converting without reading the full SQL file context
Using OSC format for databases that do not support online schema changes

faq.question: What databases support OSC format?
faq.answer: OSC is commonly used with MySQL online schema change tools. Check your database tool documentation for compatibility.

faq.question: What is the output file naming convention?
faq.answer: Output files follow the pattern osc-YYYYMMDD.txt and are placed in src/main/resources/db/osc/ directory.

faq.question: Does this skill execute SQL on my database?
faq.answer: No, this skill only transforms SQL text to OSC format. It does not connect to or modify any database.

faq.question: What SQL statements are supported?
faq.answer: Supports USE, ALTER TABLE, CREATE INDEX statements following Flyway naming conventions.

faq.question: How do I handle multiple operations on one table?
faq.answer: Comma-join operations without spaces, e.g., ADD COLUMN col1,ADD INDEX idx1 (col1);

faq.question: What encoding should the output use?
faq.answer: UTF-8 without BOM with LF line endings and TAB separators between columns.
